problem: Ticket references src/services/user-service.ts (line 45 type error: getUserById returns Promise<User | null>) and src/controllers/user-controller.ts (calling code treats return as Promise<User> without null-checking). Both files and their parent directories do not exist in the repository. No User model/schema exists either. The files must be created to introduce the described service/controller pattern with the correct null-handling.

files.why: Ticket-referenced service file containing getUserById function. Does NOT exist; must be created.
Ticket-referenced controller calling getUserById without null check. Does NOT exist; must be created.
Drizzle ORM schema definitions. Currently defines counterSchema and favoriteRestaurantSchema only. No User schema exists. A user schema may need to be added here.
Database connection singleton used by all data-access code. Exports `db` instance with Drizzle ORM.
Creates Drizzle connection with pg Pool using DATABASE_URL. Referenced schema import from @/models/Schema.
Existing API route pattern showing how db queries, validation, and NextResponse are used. No service/controller layer; logic is inline.
Existing API route showing auth (Clerk), Drizzle queries, Zod validation, and 401/422 error responses. Closest pattern to what the ticket describes.
Example Zod validation schema pattern used by existing routes.
Example Zod validation schema pattern showing multiple exports for different operations.
TypeScript config: strict: true, strictNullChecks: true. This configuration is what would surface the described type error (Promise<User | null> used as Promise<User>).
Drizzle Kit config: schema in ./src/models/Schema.ts, migrations output to ./migrations, dialect postgresql.
Quality gate commands: check:types (tsc --noEmit), lint (ultracite), test (vitest), build (db:migrate + next build). Drizzle ORM dependency confirmed.
Coding conventions: named exports only, absolute imports via @/, no default exports (except Next.js pages), TypeScript everywhere, JSDoc style guidance, test naming conventions.
Environment variable validation with @t3-oss/env-nextjs. All env access must go through Env.
Logger module using @logtape/logtape. Exported as `logger` from 'app' category.

facts: Neither src/services/ nor src/controllers/ directories exist in the repository.
No User model, schema, or type definition exists anywhere in the codebase (grep for 'getUserById' and 'User' type returned zero hits in src/).
Existing data access is done inline in Next.js API route handlers (src/app/[locale]/api/*/route.ts), not through a service/controller layer.
Drizzle ORM is used (drizzle-orm ^0.45.1, drizzle-kit ^0.31.10) with PostgreSQL via pg Pool.
Database schema is at src/models/Schema.ts, defining counterSchema and favoriteRestaurantSchema only.
Migrations are file-based: drizzle-kit generate outputs to ./migrations/, and db:migrate applies them. Build script runs db:migrate before next build.
TypeScript strict mode is enabled with strictNullChecks: true — a Promise<User | null> assigned to Promise<User> would be a compile error.
Quality gates: check:types (tsc --noEmit --pretty), lint (ultracite check --type-aware --type-check), test (vitest run), build (run-s db:migrate build:next).
Auth is handled via @clerk/nextjs; userId comes from `auth()` call (see favorite-restaurants route).
Existing patterns use NextResponse.json() for API responses with status codes (401, 422).
AGENTS.md mandates: named exports, @/ absolute imports, no default exports, sentence-case JSDoc, Zod type-only import, no unnecessary try/catch.
Test conventions: *.test.ts co-located, *.spec.ts in tests/, vitest + vitest-browser-react, describe/it pattern.
unknowns: Whether the ticket intends for these files to be created from scratch (most likely given evidence) or whether they were supposed to exist on a different branch.
What shape the User type should take — no User model or interface exists in the codebase.
Whether the user-service should query an actual users database table (requiring a schema addition and migration) or use Clerk's user data API.
Whether the controller pattern (src/controllers/) is intended to replace the existing inline API route pattern, or coexist alongside it.
Whether a new API route (e.g., src/app/[locale]/api/users/[id]/route.ts) should be created to wire the controller, or if the controller is invoked differently.
